title: nepeta incana
intro: Nepeta incana, also known as Catmint or Wood Catmint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Nepeta incana include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve anxiety and stress, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing the dried herb with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
